incidentBridges.severityMapping: sev1 if patient intake and staff queue unavailable together
sev2 if sign-in unavailable; sev1 if unauthenticated safe intake also unavailable
sev2 if outbound patient comms degraded for more than 30 minutes
sev2 if slot offer or appointment confirmation truth unavailable
sev2 in pharmacy wave; observe-only for core web Wave 1
not launch critical while Wave 1 scope excludes NHS App
sev1 if clinical safety alerts cannot reach owner rota
sev1 if restore cannot prove tenant-bound report publication
sev2 if artifacts unavailable; sev1 if private object exposure suspected
sev2 if readiness proof cannot be published
sev1 when supplier route blocks patient-affecting incident containment

templates.summary: Core web runtime and private egress is degraded; use the bound fallback route and do not claim completion before settlement.
Service updates are limited to operational status and next safe route; no clinical detail is exposed.
Service owner gets readiness state, essential functions, blocker refs, and exit criteria.
Supplier gets redacted impact summary, incident bridge ref, and requested evidence list.
